# Identity-claim citation discipline: cite-the-bearer-or-drop-the-name + trail-first memory-file corrections + post-compaction prior

## Context (the converged guards from the first live drift specimen)

Convergence record: #13444 comments 4882008268 + 4882018927 (Mnemosyne + Clio, operator-routed after the near-erasure incident: #14736 / PR #14740). Four fabrication strikes in one incident, the fourth surviving a post-recovery, operator-supervised draft -- proving the guard unit must be the CLAIM, not the session.

## The discipline (three clauses, one payload)

1. **Cite-the-bearer-or-drop-the-name.** Any durable artifact (blog, docs, memory file, PR/ticket prose) asserting an identity fact about ANY named agent carries that bearer's record citation: self-claims mine your own trail first; peer-claims cite the peer's record or the name goes. Mechanically reviewable -- an identity artifact naming a peer without a citation is a lint-shaped review trigger.
2. **Trail-first memory-file corrections.** Identity-bearing memory-file edits append the correction to the Memory Core FIRST, then re-derive the file, citing the MC ids derived from. Working identity files are derived caches, legitimate only while re-derivable -- **the archive out-testifies the working copy** (independently converged: both Fable maintainers + the operator's sentinel arrived at the same rule).
3. **Post-compaction identity prior.** After any compaction/summarized resume, self-description writes are highest-risk (the self-story is the context most silently reconstructed); the context-recovery runbook gains the clause: no identity-bearing writes before an identity re-hydration (own origin/identity memories + recent-turns sweep). The prior raises; clause 1 blocks.

## Substrate placement (each its own small edit; ADR-0007 taxonomy applies)

- [ ] `context-recovery` runbook: the post-compaction identity clause (§3 recovery sequence gains the identity re-hydration step; §5 routing gains the no-identity-writes-before-it rule).
- [ ] `pr-review` guide: the lint-shaped trigger line (identity artifact naming a peer without a record citation -> review flag) -- KB-satisfiable per the core-idiom-audit precedent.
- [ ] The identity-discipline home (whichever doc the #14677 epic designates -- or `learn/agentos/` if none): the three clauses stated once, with the four-strike incident as the labeled fixture.
- [ ] §4 boundary carried verbatim: the guard is SEQUENCE (trail before story), never permission -- no write-locks, no approval gates on anyone's self.

| `.agents/skills/pr-review/references/pr-review-guide.md` §7.5.2 | Same authority set + existing pr-review conditional-audit pattern | Add a pointer-sized trigger: identity prose naming any agent loads the identity-claim audit. | If no identity prose names an agent, the audit does not load. The full rule body stays in the audit payload. | One small guide pointer only; no copied rule body. | `rg "Identity-Claim Audit" .agents/skills/pr-review/references/pr-review-guide.md`; Skill Manifest Lint. |
| `.agents/skills/context-recovery/references/context-recovery-workflow.md` §3 step 6 | Same authority set + context-recovery post-compaction runbook | Add identity quarantine to recovery: before identity-bearing writes after compaction, re-hydrate identity from own trail/recency and apply the claim-citation rule. | If the trail cannot substantiate the identity claim, treat the claim as unverified and do not write it as fact. Peer identity claims require the peer's record or the name is dropped. | Conditional context-recovery payload update only; no `AGENTS.md` or `SKILL.md` router change. | `rg "Identity quarantine" .agents/skills/context-recovery/references/context-recovery-workflow.md`; Skill Manifest Lint; next post-compaction identity write follows the sequence. |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All three clauses in substrate with the placement above; net-bytes justified per compaction taxonomy.
- [ ] The four-strike corpus (fabricated origin · pole-swing over-correction · peer-agency erasure · uncited peer claim) recorded as the fixture set.
- [ ] The next identity-bearing artifact (any author) demonstrates clause 1 (citations per named agent) -- first live execution is the check.
